judgment.summary: Snowflake shows strong revenue growth and healthy gross margins, but is unprofitable on an operating and net basis with high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (Jan 2026) EPS of $0.32 beat estimates of $0.27 by 17.8%. Next earnings date is May 27, 2026.
positives: Revenue growth of 30.1% YoY indicates strong market demand.
Free cash flow of $1.59B TTM is positive, showing cash generation ability.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price of $229.24, suggesting upside potential.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by 17.8%.
negatives: Forward P/E of 69.95x suggests a high valuation relative to future earnings expectations.
Operating margin of -33.2% and profit margin of -28.4% indicate the company is not currently profitable.
Return on equity of -53.9% and return on assets of -9.9% show poor profitability relative to equity and assets.
Debt/Equity ratio of 142.46% is high, indicating significant leverage.
PEG ratio of 4.60x suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to its expected growth rate.

news.paragraph: Snowflake (SNOW) is trading around $169.55, with recent news indicating a potential catalyst from upcoming earnings. Bank of America has shown optimism, raising their price target to $205 from $195 and suggesting the company is poised to deliver a strong quarter. Traders considering options on SNOW should also note the ongoing discussion comparing Snowflake to DELL in the AI infrastructure space.
